raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need grep -R "foo" /system | tee? Actually output to console and then number of matched lines. Use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/stderr? Wait console is stdout. We can pipe to tee and then wc -l. But we need to display matches then count. Use grep -R "foo" /system | tee >(wc -l) ??? That prints matches then count. But tee will output to stdout and also to process substitution. That prints count after matches. So command: grep -R "foo" /system | tee >(wc -l) . That prints matches then count. Ensure count printed after. Use -n? Not needed. Provide.
